articles.headline: Google Launches TensorFlow 2.21 And LiteRT: Faster GPU Performance, New NPU Acceleration, And Seamless PyTorch Edge Deployment Upgrades
articles.description: Google has officially released TensorFlow 2.21. The most significant update in this release is the graduation of LiteRT from its preview stage to a fully production-ready stack. Moving forward, LiteRT serves as the universal on-device inference framework, officially replacing TensorFlow Lite (TFLite). This update streamlines the deployment of machine learning models to mobile and edge […] The post Google Launches TensorFlow 2.21 And LiteRT: Faster GPU Performance, New NPU Acceleration, And Seamless PyTorch Edge Deployment Upgrades appeared first on MarkTechPost.

articles.headline: Anthropic's new marketplace lets enterprise customers spend their existing AI budget on third-party tools
articles.description: Anthropic is launching the Anthropic Marketplace, a storefront where corporate customers can buy third-party software built on Anthropic's AI models. The article Anthropic's new marketplace lets enterprise customers spend their existing AI budget on third-party tools appeared first on The Decoder.

articles.description: Iran's government cut internet connectivity shortly after start of U.S.-Israeli attacks on Feb. 28.

articles.headline: ‘It means missile defence on data centres’: drone strikes raises doubts over Gulf as AI superpower
articles.description: Iran’s targeting of commercial datacentres in the UAE and Bahrain signals a new frontier in asymmetric warfareIt is believed to be a first: the deliberate targeting of a commercial datacentre by the armed forces of a country at war.At 4.30am on Sunday morning, an Iranian Shahed 136 drone struck an Amazon Web Services datacentre in the United Arab Emirates, setting off a devastating fire and forcing a shutdown of the power supply. Further damage was inflicted as attempts were made to suppress the flames with water. Continue reading...

articles.headline: Anthropic turns Claude Code into a background worker with local scheduled tasks
articles.description: Claude Code Desktop now lets users schedule recurring tasks that run automatically, like checking error logs and creating pull requests for fixable bugs every few hours. The article Anthropic turns Claude Code into a background worker with local scheduled tasks appeared first on The Decoder.

software_engineering.headline: Google Releases TensorFlow 2.21 and LiteRT for Faster Edge AI Performance
software_engineering.what_changed: Google released TensorFlow 2.21, making LiteRT the new production-ready universal on-device inference framework, replacing TensorFlow Lite.
software_engineering.why_care: This update streamlines deploying machine learning models to mobile and edge devices, potentially simplifying your AI-powered application development.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Review the TensorFlow 2.21 documentation to understand LiteRT's new capabilities and migration paths for existing TFLite projects.

software_engineering.headline: Anthropic Launches Marketplace for Enterprise AI Tools
software_engineering.what_changed: Anthropic launched a new marketplace where enterprise customers can buy third-party software built on Anthropic's AI models.
software_engineering.why_care: This marketplace could provide you with new tools and integrations that leverage Anthropic's AI, potentially accelerating your development of AI-powered enterprise solutions.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Explore the Anthropic Marketplace to see if any available tools or services can integrate with your current projects or workflows.

software_engineering.headline: Iran's Internet Blackout Enters Second Week Following Attacks
software_engineering.what_changed: Iran's government cut internet connectivity shortly after U.S.-Israeli attacks began on February 28, and the blackout has continued into its second week.
software_engineering.why_care: Internet blackouts can disrupt global services and data flows, potentially impacting your applications or services that rely on connectivity to or from affected regions.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Assess your application's resilience and disaster recovery plans for scenarios involving prolonged regional internet outages.

software_engineering.headline: Drone Strike on AWS Data Center in UAE Raises Data Security Concerns
software_engineering.what_changed: An Iranian Shahed 136 drone struck an Amazon Web Services data center in the United Arab Emirates, causing a fire and power shutdown.
software_engineering.why_care: This incident highlights the increasing physical security risks to commercial data centers, which could impact the availability and integrity of your cloud-hosted applications.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Review your cloud provider's physical security measures and your own disaster recovery strategies, including multi-region deployments, to mitigate risks from such attacks.

software_engineering.headline: Anthropic's Claude Code Desktop Now Supports Scheduled Tasks
software_engineering.what_changed: Claude Code Desktop now allows users to schedule recurring tasks, such as checking error logs and creating pull requests for bugs, to run automatically.
software_engineering.why_care: This feature can automate routine development and maintenance tasks, potentially freeing up your time for more complex problem-solving and feature development.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Experiment with Claude Code Desktop's new scheduling capabilities to automate repetitive coding tasks in your workflow this week.
